name: Swimmer Puppy Syndrome
description: Swimmer puppy syndrome is a developmental condition where neonatal puppies fail to rise up on their legs and instead lie flat with limbs splayed laterally. They paddle when attempting to move. Early intervention is critical for the best outcomes.
symptoms: Inability to stand by 3-4 weeks of age
treatment_overview: Early physiotherapy is highly effective. Splinting and taping the legs into normal position, multiple daily exercise sessions, and physiotherapy surfaces. Most puppies respond well if intervention begins before 3-4 weeks.
prevention_tips: Provide appropriate flooring with traction for puppies
Avoid excessively slippery surfaces in whelping areas
